steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage opens with the narrator predicting she will succeed and then lists her speed, size advantage, and tactical skill as support. The question asks what the passage mainly does, so the correct choice must describe this prediction-plus-evidence structure.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — comparing different sports: the passage is set entirely within one sport (basketball). No comparison between sports appears. ✗
  • Choice B — teaching how basketball is played: the narrator does not explain rules or mechanics. She is justifying a personal prediction, not instructing the reader. ✗
  • Choice C — describing how team members are selected: no selection process or tryout criteria are discussed. ✗
  • Choice D — explaining the narrator's reasoning: the narrator states her prediction (‘there's just a chance that I shall make it’) and then walks through the athletic advantages that support it. This matches the passage's structure exactly. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
